message.content:
Test file: src/game-of-life.spec.ts
Failing test: "should return empty grid for two live cells (underpopulation)"
Expected behavior: nextGeneration([[true, true]]) returns [[false, false]]
Current error: AssertionError: expected [ [ false ] ] to deeply equal [ [ false, false ] ]
Implementation file: src/game-of-life.ts

Current implementation returns [[false]] for any non-empty input. Need to make it return dead cells matching input dimensions.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: Let me read the current implementation and test files.

The test expects `nextGeneration([[true, true]])` to return `[[false, false]]`. The current implementation returns `[[false]]` for any non-empty input. The minimal fix is to make the returned grid match the input dimensions.

content.text:
All 3 active tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Changed the return from a hardcoded `[[false]]` to `grid.map(row => row.map(() => false))`, which produces a grid of dead cells matching the input dimensions.
**Result**: All tests now pass (3 passing, 4 todo)
**Approach**: This is minimal because it only does what the current test demands - return dead cells in the same shape as the input. No neighbor-counting logic was added, which future tests will require.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.

File: `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_15-35-57_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_sonnet-4-6-no-thinking/src/game-of-life.ts`
